title: Preserving Identity While Editing
concept: A key challenge is changing only what the instruction requests while keeping unrelated parts of the image (like a person's face) unchanged.

exercises.prompt: You ask an AI to change your friend's shirt to red in a photo. What should stay exactly the same?

options.text: Nothing, the whole photo can change

exercises.explain: Only the shirt should change! Everything else — face, background, pose — should stay exactly the same.

cards.title: Oops, Wrong Face!
cards.body: Imagine asking an AI to add sunglasses to your photo... and it gives you a whole new face! That's the #1 problem AI image editors try to solve: changing ONE thing without messing up everything else.

cards.title: What Is Identity Preservation?
cards.body: Identity preservation means an AI editor keeps a person's face, body, and other unrequested details unchanged while only editing the specific part you asked for, like clothing, background, or color.

cards.title: Like a Careful Barber
cards.body: Think of a skilled barber trimming just your bangs. They don't shave your whole head! A good AI editor works the same way — it snips only the part you asked about and leaves the rest of 'you' untouched.

cards.title: Real Example
cards.body: Instruction: 'Change the shirt to blue.' A good edit: same face, same hair, same background — only the shirt color changes. A bad edit: the face looks slightly different, like a different person entirely.

cards.title: Myth: More Changes = Better AI
cards.body: Myth: A powerful AI should transform the whole image dramatically. Truth: The best AI editors are precise, not dramatic. Changing only what's asked — and nothing else — is actually much harder and smarter than changing everything.

cards.title: Good Edit vs Bad Edit
cards.body: Same instruction, two different results — only one keeps the person's identity intact.

left.text: Face, hair, and background stay identical — only the requested shirt color changes.

right.text: The face looks different, like a new person — even though only the shirt was requested.

exercises.prompt: You ask an AI to 'add a hat' to a photo. What counts as a successful edit?

options.text: A hat appears, everything else stays the same
A hat appears, but the face changes slightly
The background changes to a beach
The person's hair color changes too

exercises.explain: A successful, identity-preserving edit only adds what was requested (the hat) — nothing else should change.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A good AI image editor should be free to change any part of the image as long as it looks nice.
exercises.explain: False! A good AI editor should only change what the instruction asks for, preserving everything else, even if changing more might 'look nice.'

exercises.sentence: Keeping a person's face and other details unchanged while editing only the requested part is called identity ___.
exercises.explain: Identity preservation is the goal of keeping everything the same except what was specifically asked to change.

exercises.prompt: An AI is asked to 'change the background to a park.' Which result shows the BEST identity preservation?

options.text: Same person, same pose, same clothes — only background is now a park
Different-looking person standing in a park
Same person, but now wearing different clothes, standing in a park

exercises.explain: Only the background was requested to change, so the person, pose, and clothes should all remain exactly the same.

exercises.prompt: Final Challenge: You instruct an AI, 'Make the sky in this photo sunset orange.' The AI returns a photo where the sky is orange, but the person's face now looks subtly different than the original. What went wrong?

options.text: The AI failed at identity preservation by changing something that wasn't requested
Nothing went wrong, the sky color is correct
The AI should have changed the person's clothes too
The instruction was too vague to understand

exercises.explain: The sky color changed correctly, but the AI also altered the person's face — something never requested. This is a classic identity preservation failure: unrelated details should always stay untouched.
